---
title: "Data Preparation and Cleaning"
format: pdf
---

## Loading Data & State Filtering

*Note: This step is run once to reduce the primary 11M+ row dataset down to a workable, state-specific file.*

```{r extract-ny-data}
#| eval: false

# Load the raw national dataset and filter exclusively for New York records.
# Saved to a compressed parquet file to optimize storage.

loans <- read_parquet("2023_public_loans.parquet")

loan_records_NY <- loans |>
  filter(state_code == "NY")

write_parquet(loan_records_NY, "loan_records_NY.parquet")
```

## Environment Setup

```{r setup}
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(skimr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(gtsummary)
library(arrow)
```



## Data Cleaning & Feature Engineering

```{r data-clean}

# LOAD STATE DATA
loans <- read_parquet("data/loan_records_NY.parquet")

# Define target columns

cols_of_interest <- c(
  "lei", "census_tract", "county_code", "state_code", 
  "action_taken", 
  "income", "debt_to_income_ratio", "combined_loan_to_value_ratio", 
  
  "loan_amount", "loan_type", "loan_purpose", 
  "property_value", "occupancy_type", "loan_term", 
  
  "derived_race", "derived_sex", "applicant_age", 
  "tract_minority_population_percent", "ffiec_msa_md_median_family_income",
  "tract_to_msa_income_percentage"
)

# FILTERING
# Isolate standard, first-lien, conventional mortgages for primary residences.
# Exclude incomplete demographic data, reverse mortgages, and commercial loans.

loans_NY <- loans |>
  filter(action_taken %in% c(1, 2, 3),
         derived_loan_product_type == "Conventional:First Lien",
         loan_purpose == 1, 
         occupancy_type == 1,
         !derived_race %in% c("Race Not Available", "Free Form Text Only"),
         derived_sex != "Sex Not Available",
         reverse_mortgage == 2, 
         business_or_commercial_purpose == 2,
         total_units %in% c(1,2),
         loan_amount < 1e7) |>
  select(all_of(cols_of_interest)) |> 
  drop_na()

# FEATURE ENGINEERING & TYPE CONVERSION

loans_NY_clean1 <- loans_NY |>
  mutate(
    # Binary outcome: 1 = Denied, 0 = Approved
    denied = ifelse(action_taken == 3, 1, 0),
    
    # Create high-level DTI risk bins based on widely accepted DTI ratio ranges
    dti_4bins = case_when(
      debt_to_income_ratio %in% c("<20%", "20%-<30%", "30%-<36%") ~ "<36%",
      debt_to_income_ratio %in% as.character(36:49) ~ "36%-49%",
      debt_to_income_ratio == "50%-60%" ~ "50%-60%",
      debt_to_income_ratio == ">60%" ~ ">60%",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # Extract numeric values from string ratios/values
    combined_loan_to_value_ratio_num = readr::parse_number(combined_loan_to_value_ratio),
    property_value_num = readr::parse_number(property_value),
    
    # Standardize race categories
    race_factor = case_when(
      derived_race == "White" ~ "White",
      derived_race == "Black or African American" ~ "Black",
      derived_race == "Asian" ~ "Asian",
      derived_race == "Joint" ~ "Joint",
      derived_race %in% c("American Indian or Alaska Native", 
                          "2 or more minority races", 
                          "Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander") ~ "Other/Multiracial",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    # Standardize age (8888 indicates missing/unknown)
    age_factor = ifelse(applicant_age == "8888", NA, applicant_age)
  )

# OUTLIER HANDLING & INSTITUTION FILTERING

# Calculate 1st and 99th percentiles to trim extreme CLTV and Income outliers
cltv_bounds <- quantile(loans_NY_clean1$combined_loan_to_value_ratio_num, probs = c(0.01, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)
p_low <- 0 
p_high <- quantile(loans_NY_clean1$income, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)
 
# Restrict analysis to institutions (LEI) with a meaningful sample size (>= 50 applications)
loans_NY_clean2 <- loans_NY_clean1 |>
  group_by(lei) |>
  filter(n() >= 50) |> 
  ungroup() 

# EXPORT

# Apply outlier thresholds, handle transformations, and enforce factor levels
loans_NY_clean3 <- loans_NY_clean2 |>
  filter(
    !is.na(race_factor), 
    !is.na(age_factor),
    combined_loan_to_value_ratio_num >= cltv_bounds[1], 
    combined_loan_to_value_ratio_num <= cltv_bounds[2],
    income > p_low, 
    income <= p_high
  ) |>
  mutate(
    # Log transformations for heavily skewed financial variables
    log_income = log(income),
    log_loan_amount = log(loan_amount),
    
    # Factor levels
    race_factor = factor(race_factor, levels = c("White", "Black", "Asian", "Joint", "Other/Multiracial")),
    age_factor = factor(applicant_age, levels = c("<25", "25-34", "35-44", "45-54", "55-64", "65-74", ">74"), ordered = TRUE),
    sex_factor = factor(derived_sex, levels = c("Male", "Female", "Joint")),
    status_factor = factor(ifelse(denied == 1, "Denied", "Approved"), levels = c("Approved", "Denied")),
    
    # Ordered factor levels for bins
    dti_4bins = factor(dti_4bins, levels = c("<36%", "36%-49%", "50%-60%", ">60%"), ordered = TRUE),
    
    # POC Flag
    applicant_poc = ifelse(race_factor == "White", "White", "POC/Non-White")
  )

# Save the final analytical dataset
write_parquet(loans_NY_clean3, "data/loans_NY_clean.parquet")
```
